params.title: Favorite Lunch Foods
params.description: Horizontal bar graph appears. Title: Favorite Lunch Foods. Scale of 5. Pizza=45, Tacos=25, Salad=10, Burgers=15.

beats.text: In Warmup, you read graphs with different scales. Now let's use graphs to solve problems. Here's data about students' favorite lunch foods.

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph showing Favorite Lunch Foods data. Scale of 5. Pizza=45, Tacos=25, Salad=10, Burgers=15.

beats.text: First, a quick check on something you learned before.

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph showing Favorite Lunch Foods data. Scale of 5. Pizza=45, Tacos=25, Salad=10, Burgers=15.

params.title: Favorite Lunch Foods
params.description: Horizontal bar graph appears. Favorite Lunch Foods data. Pizza=45, Tacos=25, Salad=10, Burgers=15. Scale of 5.

beats.text: How many more students chose pizza than salad? Remember what how many more tells us to do.
How many more students chose pizza than salad?

validator.description: Student selected 35, correct answer from subtraction 45 minus 10

beats.text: Right. 35 more. How many more means compare. We subtract. Pizza is 45, salad is 10, so 45 minus 10 is 35.

validator.description: Student selected 55, which is the sum of pizza and salad instead of the difference

beats.text: How many more asks us to compare. When we compare, we subtract to find the difference. How many more is pizza than salad?

validator.description: Student selected 30, possibly confusing categories or misreading values

beats.text: Check the values carefully. Pizza is 45. Salad is 10. To find how many more, subtract the smaller from the larger.

validator.description: Student selected 15, which is the value of burgers, not the difference between pizza and salad

beats.text: How many more means we compare pizza to salad. Pizza is 45, salad is 10. Subtract to find the difference.

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Animation demonstrates comparing Pizza bar (45) to Salad bar (10), showing subtraction 45 minus 10 equals 35.

beats.text: Let me show you. How many more tells us to compare by subtracting. Pizza is 45. Salad is 10. We subtract: 45 minus 10 equals 35. The answer is 35 because that's the difference between pizza and salad.

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph in reading mode. Favorite Lunch Foods data. Pizza=45, Tacos=25, Salad=10, Burgers=15. Scale of 5. Multiple choice tool active.
elements.title: Favorite Lunch Foods

beats.text: Good. How many more means compare, and compare means subtract. You've got that. Now here's something new: What if the problem asks about more than one category?

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph titled Favorite Lunch Foods in reading mode. Scale 5. Pizza=45, Tacos=25, Salad=10, Burgers=15.

params.title: Favorite Lunch Foods
params.description: Horizontal bar graph appears. Favorite Lunch Foods. Scale 5. Pizza=45, Tacos=25, Salad=10, Burgers=15. Question displayed: How many more students chose pizza than tacos and salad together?

beats.text: Remember this question from Warmup? You noticed it needed more than one step. Now let's figure out HOW to solve it.

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph. Favorite Lunch Foods. Scale 5. Pizza=45, Tacos=25, Salad=10, Burgers=15. Question displayed on screen.

beats.text: First, let's find the categories we need. Which categories does the question ask about? Select all of the needed categories.
Which categories do we need?

validator.description: Student selected Pizza, Tacos, and Salad (3 correct categories)

beats.text: Right. Those three. Notice we don't need burgers. The question doesn't ask about burgers.

validator.description: Student selected an incomplete or incorrect set of categories on first attempt

beats.text: Read the question again. Which foods does it mention?

validator.description: Student selected an incomplete or incorrect set of categories on second attempt

params.description: Pizza, Tacos, and Salad bars highlight.

beats.text: The question compares pizza to tacos and salad together. That means we need Pizza, Tacos, and Salad. Which three categories are those?

validator.description: Student selected an incomplete or incorrect set of categories. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System highlights Pizza, Tacos, and Salad bars in sequence, then all three together.

beats.text: Let me show you. The question asks how many more students chose pizza than tacos and salad together. That tells us we need three categories: Pizza, Tacos, and Salad. We don't need Burgers because the question doesn't mention burgers. The three categories we need are Pizza, Tacos, and Salad.

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph. Favorite Lunch Foods. Scale 5. Pizza=45, Tacos=25, Salad=10, Burgers=15. Multi-select tool active with all four categories as options.

params.title: Favorite Lunch Foods
params.description: Horizontal bar graph appears. Favorite Lunch Foods data. Pizza 45, Tacos 25, Salad 10, Burgers 15. Scale of 5.

beats.text: Let me show you how I solve this. First, I ask myself: What should I find first?

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph. Favorite Lunch Foods. Pizza 45, Tacos 25, Salad 10, Burgers 15. Scale 5. Reading mode.
elements.title: Favorite Lunch Foods

beats.text: The question says tacos and salad together. That word together tells me to add those first. Let me find that total.

params.description: Tacos and Salad bars highlight together.

beats.text: Tacos is 25, salad is 10. Combined, that's 35.

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph. Favorite Lunch Foods. Tacos and Salad bars highlighted. Calculation 25 + 10 = 35 visible.
elements.title: Favorite Lunch Foods

beats.text: Now I can compare. How many more is pizza than that total of 35?
Pizza is 45 while tacos and salad combined is 35. 45 minus 35 is 10. Pizza is 10 more than tacos and salad combined.

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph. Favorite Lunch Foods. Pizza bar highlighted. Comparison shown: 45 vs 35.
elements.title: Favorite Lunch Foods

params.description: Strategy statement appears on screen: 'Find the combined total first, then compare.'

beats.text: Here's the strategy: When you see 'combined' or 'together' or 'altogether,' find the total of those categories first. Then compare to the other given category.

elements.description: Strategy statement displayed on screen: 'Find the combined total first, then compare.'

beats.text: Find the combined total first, then compare. That's the key to these problems. What do we find first when we see 'combined'?
What do we find first when we see 'combined'?

validator.description: Student selected 'The total', correct answer

beats.text: Right. Find the total by adding. That's always our first step when we see 'combined.'

validator.description: Student selected 'The difference': confusing the second step (comparing) with the first step (finding the combined total)

params.description: Strategy statement pulses to draw attention to the first step.

beats.text: The difference comes later. When we see 'combined,' we add first. Look at the strategy: what's the first thing we do?

validator.description: Student selected 'The largest number': misunderstanding that 'combined' signals addition, not identifying an extreme value

params.description: The word 'combined' highlights on the strategy statement.

beats.text: Combined means put together, not pick the biggest. When we see 'combined,' we add the categories. What do we get when we add?

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Animation highlights 'Find the combined total first' on the strategy statement.

beats.text: Let me show you. Combined means add. When we see 'combined' or 'together,' we find the total by adding those categories. That's always the first step. The answer is the total.

elements.description: Strategy statement displayed on screen: 'Find the combined total first, then compare.'

params.title: Favorite Ways to Roll
params.description: Vertical bar graph appears. Scale of 10. Title: Favorite Ways to Roll. Skateboards=15, Rollerblades=10, Bikes=65, Scooters=30.

beats.text: Your turn. Here's a new graph.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Favorite Ways to Roll. Scale 10. Skateboards=15, Rollerblades=10, Bikes=65, Scooters=30.

params.description: Question text appears: How many more students chose bikes than scooters and skateboards together?

beats.text: This problem needs two steps. What should we find first?
Select what to find first.

validator.description: Student selected to add scooters and skateboards, correct first step

beats.text: That's it. Scooters and skateboards combined. Let's find that total.

validator.description: Student selected Compare bikes to scooters instead of finding the combined total first

params.description: Scooters and Skateboards bars highlight together.

beats.text: The question says scooters and skateboards together. That word together tells us to add those first. What's the combined total?

validator.description: Student selected Add all four categories instead of the specific categories mentioned in the question

beats.text: We only need the categories the question asks about. The question says scooters and skateboards together. Find the total of those two.

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Scooters and Skateboards bars highlight and pulse together.

beats.text: Let me show you. The question says scooters and skateboards together. Together means add those first. That's the strategy: find the combined total first. The answer is Add scooters and skateboards.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Favorite Ways to Roll. Scale 10. Question displayed. Multiple choice active.

params.description: Scooters bar (30) and Skateboards bar (15) highlight.

beats.text: Scooters and skateboards combined is 45. Now we compare. How many more is bikes than 45?
How many more is bikes than 45?

validator.description: Student selected 20, correct answer (65 − 45 = 20)

beats.text: Right. 20 more. Bikes is 65, combined is 45, so 65 minus 45 is 20. You used the strategy: find the combined total first, then compare.

validator.description: Student selected 110, added bikes to the combined total instead of subtracting

beats.text: How many more means compare. We subtract, not add. Bikes is 65. The combined total is 45. How many more is 65 than 45?

validator.description: Student selected 65, the value for bikes alone without comparing

beats.text: That's how many chose bikes. The question asks how many more. We need to compare 65 to the combined total of 45. Subtract to find the difference.

validator.description: Student selected 45, the combined total instead of the difference

params.description: Bikes bar highlights above the combined value.

beats.text: That's the combined total of scooters and skateboards. Now we compare. Bikes is 65. How many more is 65 than 45?

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System shows bikes bar (65) compared to combined value (45), then displays 65 minus 45 equals 20.

beats.text: Let me show you. Scooters and skateboards combined is 45. Bikes is 65. How many more means subtract. 65 minus 45 is 20. That's the difference. The answer is 20.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Favorite Ways to Roll. Scale 10. Scooters and Skateboards bars highlighted. Calculation 30 + 15 = 45 shown. Multiple choice active for second step.

beats.text: You're getting it. Find the combined total first, then compare. Let's practice more, and I'll give you less help each time.

params.title: Fruit Stand Sales
params.description: Horizontal bar graph appears. Fruit Stand Sales. Scale of 5. Grapes=15, Apples=45, Bananas=30, Oranges=25.

beats.text: Here's another one. What should we find first?
Select what to find first.

validator.description: Student selected add oranges and grapes, correct strategy

beats.text: Right. Oranges and grapes combined. Find that total.

validator.description: Student selected subtract grapes from oranges

beats.text: The question says oranges and grapes together. Together means add, not subtract. What's the total?

validator.description: Student selected compare apples to oranges

beats.text: Not yet. The question asks about oranges and grapes combined. Find that total first, then you can compare.

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System highlights Oranges and Grapes together and displays equation: 25 + 15 = 40

beats.text: When we see oranges and grapes together, that tells us to add first. Oranges is 25, grapes is 15, so 25 plus 15 equals 40. That combined total is what we compare to apples. The answer is add oranges and grapes.

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph in reading mode. Fruit Stand Sales. Scale 5. Grapes=15, Apples=45, Bananas=30, Oranges=25. Multiple choice tool active.
elements.title: Fruit Stand Sales

params.description: Oranges bar (25) and Grapes bar (15) highlight.

beats.text: Oranges is 25. Grapes is 15. What's the combined total?
How many oranges and grapes combined?

validator.description: Student answered 40, correct (25 + 15)

beats.text: That's right. 40 combined.

validator.description: Student selected 10, which is the difference between oranges and grapes

beats.text: You subtracted oranges and grapes. The question asks for the total. Add them: 25 plus 15 equals what?

validator.description: Student selected 30, possibly counting Bananas

beats.text: Look at the highlighted bars. Oranges is 25, grapes is 15. Add those two. What's 25 plus 15?

validator.description: Student selected 45, which is the value for Apples

beats.text: That's apples. We need oranges and grapes combined. Oranges is 25, grapes is 15. Add them.

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System highlights Oranges and Grapes and displays equation: 25 + 15 = 40

beats.text: Let me show you. Oranges is 25. Grapes is 15. When we add them, 25 plus 15 equals 40. That's the combined total we need. The answer is 40.

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph. Fruit Stand Sales. Scale 5. Oranges and Grapes bars highlighted. Values: Grapes=15, Apples=45, Bananas=30, Oranges=25. Multiple choice tool active.
elements.title: Fruit Stand Sales

beats.text: Oranges and grapes combined is 40. Apples is 45. Now find how many fewer.
How many fewer oranges and grapes combined than apples?

validator.description: Student answered 5, correct (45 - 40)

beats.text: That's it. 5 fewer. You found the combined total first, then compared.

validator.description: Student selected 85, which is the sum of the combined total and apples

beats.text: You added. The question asks how many fewer, which means subtract. The combined total is 40. Apples is 45. Find the difference.

validator.description: Student selected 45, which is the value for Apples

beats.text: That's apples. We already know apples is 45. Now compare: the combined total is 40, and apples is 45. How many fewer is 40 than 45?

validator.description: Student selected 40, which is the combined total

beats.text: That's the combined total. We need to compare it to apples. Apples is 45, combined is 40. Subtract to find how many fewer.

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System highlights Apples bar and displays: 45 compared to 40, showing difference calculation 45 minus 40 equals 5

beats.text: Here's how to compare. The combined total of oranges and grapes is 40. Apples is 45. To find how many fewer, we subtract: 45 minus 40 equals 5. Oranges and grapes combined is 5 fewer than apples. The answer is 5.

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph. Fruit Stand Sales. Scale 5. Apples bar highlighted. Values: Grapes=15, Apples=45, Bananas=30, Oranges=25. Multiple choice tool active.
elements.title: Fruit Stand Sales

params.title: Pets at the Shelter
params.description: Horizontal bar graph appears. Pets at the Shelter. Scale of 10. Dogs=65, Cats=40, Birds=25, Fish=30.

beats.text: One more. Think about what to do first.

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph titled Pets at the Shelter. Scale 10. Dogs=65, Cats=40, Birds=25, Fish=30. Reading mode.

beats.text: How many more dogs are there than fish and birds combined?
Select what to find first.

validator.description: Student selected Add fish and birds, correct first step

beats.text: Right. Fish and birds combined. Find that total.

validator.description: Student selected Compare dogs to fish, skipping the combined step

beats.text: The question says fish and birds combined. That word combined tells us to add those two first.

validator.description: Student selected Subtract birds from dogs, missing the combined step

beats.text: Look at the question again. It asks about fish and birds combined. When you see combined, find that total first.

validator.description: Student selected Add all pets, including categories not needed

beats.text: We don't need all the pets. The question only asks about fish and birds combined. Which categories does the question mention?

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Fish and Birds bars highlight, then Dogs bar highlights.

beats.text: Let me show you. The question says fish and birds combined. Combined means add those first. Fish is 30, birds is 25, so 30 plus 25 is 55. Now we compare. Dogs is 65, combined is 55, so 65 minus 55 is 10. The answer is 10 more dogs. The strategy is: find the combined total first, then compare.

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph titled Pets at the Shelter. Scale 10. Dogs=65, Cats=40, Birds=25, Fish=30. Reading mode. Multiple choice tool active.

params.description: Fish bar (30) and Birds bar (25) highlight.

beats.text: Fish is 30. Birds is 25. What's the combined total of fish and birds?
How many fish and birds combined?

validator.description: Student selected 55, correct sum of 30 + 25

beats.text: Right. 55 combined.

validator.description: Student selected 5, subtracted fish and birds instead of adding

beats.text: The question asks for fish and birds combined. Combined means add. What is 30 plus 25?

validator.description: Student selected 45, incorrect calculation

beats.text: Count carefully. Fish is 30, birds is 25. Add those numbers. What is 30 plus 25?

validator.description: Student selected 65, gave the dogs value instead of the combined total

beats.text: That's the number of dogs. We need fish and birds combined. Look at those two bars. What is 30 plus 25?

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Fish bar (30) and Birds bar (25) highlight, calculation 30 + 25 = 55 displays.

beats.text: Here's how to find it. Fish is 30. Birds is 25. When we add them, 30 plus 25 is 55. The combined total is 55.

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph titled Pets at the Shelter. Scale 10. Fish and Birds bars highlighted. Dogs=65, Cats=40, Birds=25, Fish=30. Reading mode. Multiple choice tool active.

beats.text: Fish and birds combined is 55. Now find how many more dogs there are.
How many more dogs than fish and birds combined?

validator.description: Student selected 10, correct difference of 65 minus 55

beats.text: Right. 10 more dogs. You found the combined total first, then compared.

validator.description: Student selected 120, added dogs to the combined total instead of subtracting

beats.text: The question asks how many MORE. How many more means compare, and compare means subtract. What is 65 minus 55?

validator.description: Student selected 65, gave the dogs value instead of the difference

beats.text: That's how many dogs there are. The question asks how many MORE dogs than the combined total. Dogs is 65, combined is 55. Find the difference.

validator.description: Student selected 55, gave the combined total instead of the difference

beats.text: That's the combined total of fish and birds. Now we need to compare. Dogs is 65, combined is 55. How many more is 65 than 55?

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Dogs bar highlights, comparison shows 65 vs 55, calculation 65 minus 55 = 10 displays.

beats.text: Let me show you the final step. We found that fish and birds combined is 55. Dogs is 65. Now we compare. How many more means subtract. 65 minus 55 is 10. The answer is 10 more dogs. This shows the strategy: find the combined total first, then compare to answer the question.

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph titled Pets at the Shelter. Scale 10. Dogs bar highlighted. Combined value 55 displayed. Dogs=65, Cats=40, Birds=25, Fish=30. Reading mode. Multiple choice tool active.

params.title: Pets at the Shelter
params.description: Horizontal bar graph appears. Pets at the Shelter data. Dogs=65, Cats=40, Birds=25, Fish=30. Scale of 10.

beats.text: How about this one? How many more dogs are there than cats?
How many more dogs are there than cats?

validator.description: Student selected 25, correct answer (65 - 40 = 25)

params.description: Dogs and Cats bars highlight to confirm selection.

beats.text: Right. 25 more dogs than cats. This one was just one step. No combined, so straight to comparing.

validator.description: Student selected 105 (added dogs and cats instead of finding the difference)

beats.text: You added dogs and cats together. But how many more asks us to compare. Find the difference between the two bars.

validator.description: Student selected 65 (dogs value only, did not subtract)

beats.text: That's the number of dogs. But how many more means compare. How far apart are dogs and cats?

validator.description: Student selected 30 (possibly confused categories or misread values)

beats.text: Check the bars for dogs and cats. Dogs is 65, cats is 40. What's the difference?

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Animation shows comparison: dogs bar at 65, cats bar at 40, with difference of 25 highlighted.

beats.text: Let me show you. How many more means find the difference. Dogs is 65. Cats is 40. To compare, we subtract: 65 minus 40 is 25. The answer is 25 more dogs than cats.

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph. Pets at the Shelter. Dogs and Cats bars highlighted. Dogs=65, Cats=40, Birds=25, Fish=30. Scale of 10.

params.title: Favorite Colors
params.description: Vertical bar graph appears. Favorite Colors data. Scale of 5. Yellow=20, Red=45, Green=25, Purple=15, Blue=45.

beats.text: Last one before we check what you know.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph titled Favorite Colors in reading mode. Scale 5. Yellow=20, Red=45, Green=25, Purple=15, Blue=45.

beats.text: How many more students chose red than chose green or yellow together? What do you find first?
Select what to find first.

validator.description: Student selected add green and yellow, correct first step

beats.text: Right. Total green and yellow first.

validator.description: Student selected compare red to green, skipping the combined total step

beats.text: The question says green or yellow together. That word together means add those first. What's the combined total?

validator.description: Student selected add all colors, adding unnecessary categories

beats.text: We only need the categories the question asks about. The question says green or yellow. Add just those two.

validator.description: Student selected subtract yellow from green, wrong operation for combined

beats.text: The question says green or yellow together. Together means add, not subtract. Find the total of green and yellow.

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

beats.text: Let me show you. The question says green or yellow together, so we add those first. Green is 25, yellow is 20. Combined, that's 45. Now we compare. Red is also 45. So the difference is zero. The first step when we see together is always to find that combined total.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph titled Favorite Colors in reading mode. Scale 5. Multiple choice tool active.

beats.text: Green and yellow combined is 45. Now compare to red.
How many more students chose red than green and yellow combined?

validator.description: Student selected 0, correct, equal values

beats.text: That's it. Red is 45, combined is 45. They're equal. No difference.

validator.description: Student selected 90, added instead of subtracting

beats.text: How many more means compare. Compare means subtract. Red is 45, combined is 45. What's the difference?

validator.description: Student selected 45, one of the values instead of the difference

beats.text: We need the difference, not one of the values. Red is 45, combined is 45. How many more is red than combined?

validator.description: Student selected 10, incorrect calculation

beats.text: Look closely at the values. Red is 45. Green and yellow combined is 45. When two values are equal, the difference is zero.

validator.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Visual comparison displays: Red 45 compared to combined 45, difference is 0

beats.text: Here's how to think about it. We found that green and yellow combined is 45. Now we compare to red. Red is also 45. When we subtract equal values, we get zero. 45 minus 45 is zero. So red and the combined total are equal. There is no difference. The answer is zero.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph titled Favorite Colors in reading mode. Scale 5. Green and Yellow bars highlighted. Calculation 25 + 20 = 45 displayed. Multiple choice tool active.

params.description: Strategy statement appears on screen: "Find the combined total first, then compare."

beats.text: You've got the strategy: find the combined total first, then compare. Let's see what you know.

elements.description: Strategy statement displayed: "Find the combined total first, then compare."
